commits.subject: fix(deps): align @types/react-dom with react-dom@19.1 runtime
commits.body:
Vercel build was failing with ERESOLVE: @types/react-dom@19.2.3 peer-
requires @types/react@^19.2.0, but the root project pins @types/react
to ~19.1.10 (which resolves to 19.1.17). Vercel's npm enforces peer
deps strictly; local resolved more leniently and masked the conflict.

Root cause: I added @types/react-dom@^19.2.3 in commit 7f6cd39 (the
DEBUG_SYSTEM hardening that introduced `react-dom/client` createRoot
in utils/debugRootMount.ts) without realigning @types/react. The
right fix isn't to bump @types/react forward — runtime is pinned to
react@19.1.0 and react-dom@19.1.0, and the type definitions should
match the runtime, not float a minor ahead. Pinned @types/react-dom
to ~19.1.11 (latest 19.1.x) instead.

After change:
- @types/react-dom@19.1.11 peer-requires @types/react@^19.0.0
- @types/react resolves to 19.1.17 (~19.1.10 range)
- Peer requirement satisfied

commits.subject: fix(edges): context-aware update toast + document dark-mode contract
commits.body:
Two items from the `edges branch` trigger:

1. handleCheckForUpdates 'update-available' toast now branches on
   isInstalled. The header Update button only renders when
   `Platform.OS === 'web' && isInstalled && hasUpdate` (home.tsx:620),
   so a non-installed web user (browser-tab, not installed PWA) who
   triggers the manual check and gets `update-available` would
   previously see "tap Update in the header" — but the header has no
   such button to tap. Now:
     - isInstalled: "An update is available — tap Update in the header"
     - not installed: "An update is available — refresh the page to apply"
   Network-first SW caching means a hard reload reliably picks up the
   new bundle for browser users, so refresh is the correct affordance.
   Updated docs/TODO.md verification scenario #7 to cover both branches.

2. Documented the storage contract for `synctone_dark_mode` in the
   theme flash-prevention inline script comment. Only three valid
   states: absent, 'true', 'false'. Any other value silently maps to
   light mode because the comparison is `darkRaw === 'true'`. The app
   only ever writes 'true'/'false' via AsyncStorage's web backend, so
   the silent fallback is operationally safe — but if a future caller
   writes a different shape (e.g. 'TRUE' from a refactor or a
   third-party migration tool), the silent-light-fallback would mask a
   real bug. Comment instructs maintainers to fix the writer rather
   than relax the check.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed

commits.subject: refactor(types): runtime-validate icon hashes + drop dead casts
commits.body:
Three type-hygiene items from the `types branch` trigger:

1. hooks/usePWAInstall.ts: removed two dead-weight casts.
   `(window as Window & { __pwaPromptReceived?: boolean })` and
   `(navigator as Navigator & { standalone?: boolean })` were both
   made redundant by the `types/rn-web-style.d.ts` module augmentations
   in commit d314346 — those ambient declarations already add the
   properties to the base Window/Navigator types. Pre-existing casts
   became dead weight once my own infrastructure landed; closing the
   loop.

2. app/+html.tsx: replaced the bare `JSON.parse(raw) as Record<string,
   string>` cast with a `parseIconHashes()` helper that validates the
   parsed shape at runtime. Top-level must be a JSON object (not
   null/array/primitive); every value must be a string. Throws with
   a specific "got <actualType>" error + remediation (`Regenerate
   with node scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs`) if either check fails.
   Catches the "someone hand-edited icon-hashes.json to an invalid
   shape" case that a bare type assertion would miss — the failure
   would otherwise surface as a cryptic downstream `iconUrl()` throw.

3. components/debug/DebugPill.tsx: extracted the per-source color
   override map out of the PALETTE object literal. The inline
   `{ ... } as Record<string, string>` inside `as const` was a
   self-contradicting cast (outer wants literal keys, inner wants
   widened-string keys). Moved to a dedicated top-level
   `SOURCE_PALETTE: Record<string, string> = { ... }` that uses
   variable-level type annotation instead of assertion. Callers
   updated; no semantic change.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed
- Unit-tested parseIconHashes against 7 input variants:
  - null → throws "got null"
  - string → throws "got string"
  - number → throws "got number"
  - array → throws "got array"
  - object with number value → throws "value must be a string, got number"
  - object with null value → throws "value must be a string, got null"
  - valid {string: string} → parses correctly
- Expo export with malformed icon-hashes.json aborts the build
  (confirmed via `npx expo export -p web`)

commits.subject: fix(race): DebugPill removes fallback pill directly + sync gate on update check
commits.body:
Two concurrency fixes surfaced by the `race branch` trigger:

(1) DebugPill's React mount didn't directly remove the inline
pre-React fallback pill — it relied on the inline script's 250ms
setInterval poll to detect `__debugMounted = true` and remove the
DOM node. That poller is cleared at 30s. If the JS bundle loads
slower than that (throttled network, cold mobile CPU, dev-mode
source maps on a large bundle), the React pill mounts AFTER the
poller stopped, leaving the fallback pill attached forever. Both
pills then render at the same top-right position.

Fix: React pill's useEffect now removes `#__pre-react-debug-pill`
directly, once and idempotently, regardless of whether the poller
is still running. In the fast-mount happy path this is a no-op
(poller removed it first); in the slow-bundle case it's the only
thing that removes the fallback.

(2) SettingsModal → Check for Updates button could fire two
concurrent `checkForUpdates()` calls on a fast double-tap. Pressable
`disabled={isCheckingForUpdates}` + the JS-level `if
(isCheckingForUpdates) return` gate both read a React-state prop
propagated through (hook → home → modal → prop → Pressable). Within
one render tick, the second tap can pass both gates before
`setIsChecking(true)` has re-rendered. Result: two concurrent SW
update checks, two Promises, two "You're up to date" toasts.

Fix: added a `updateCheckInFlight = useRef(false)` gate that flips
synchronously inside `handleCheckForUpdates`. Two taps in the same
event-loop tick are serialized — the second reads the synchronous
ref value (true) and returns early. Ref is released in `finally` so
a failed check doesn't wedge the button. The existing state-based
gates remain as belt-and-braces at the Pressable layer.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed

commits.subject: fix(errors): surface silent background failures — 2 error-path gaps
commits.body:
Two error-path gaps found by the `errors branch` trigger. Both were
introduced on this branch; fixing them symmetrically with existing
error-surface patterns in the same files.

- hooks/usePWAUpdate.ts runPeriodicUpdateCheck: the hourly
  `_registration?.update().catch(() => {})` swallowed SW-update
  failures with no trace. Developers investigating "why isn't my
  update getting detected on the hourly poll?" had nothing to look
  at. Replaced with `.catch((err) => debugLog('pwa',
  'periodic-sw-update-error', String(err), 'warn'))` — symmetric with
  the manual-check path's 'manual-check-error-sw' log and the
  version.json path's 'version-check-error' log. Background polling
  still never throws or breaks the page.

- scripts/check-player-ids.ts top-level `checkPlayerIds()` call had
  no `.catch`. Inner Supabase errors are properly guarded via
  `if (error)` checks, but a rejected Promise from the client itself
  (network failure, TLS error, CORS from a misconfigured proxy)
  would propagate to Node 22's default UnhandledPromiseRejection
  handler — ugly stack, non-specific error. Wrapped with explicit
  catch that prints a single-line message + stack (if Error
  instance) and exits 1 so wrapper scripts / CI can detect failure.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed
- EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY="" npx tsx scripts/check-player-ids.ts:
  still hits the existing early-return guard cleanly
- Simulated top-level throw: catch fires, prints "❌ Unexpected error:
  <message>", exit code 1

commits.subject: fix(bugs): reversed-marker guard + clipboard textarea cleanup
commits.body:
Two small correctness fixes surfaced by the `bugs branch` trigger:

- scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs rewriteSwIconList: added
  `endIdx <= beginIdx` guard. Without it, if someone accidentally
  swaps the `@icon-cache-bust:begin` / `:end` markers in sw.js, both
  `indexOf` calls succeed but the slice logic (`before + newBlock +
  after`) produces silently-garbage output — no error, stale PWA
  icons ship. Verified: reversing the markers now throws "sw.js
  markers are in the wrong order" with specific offsets, aborting
  the build. Byte-identical output on the normal path is preserved
  (verified by post-fix run).

- components/debug/DebugPill.tsx copyToClipboardWithFallback: wrapped
  the textarea append→remove window in try/finally. Previously, if
  any call between appendChild and removeChild threw (most
  realistically `execCommand('copy')` in ancient browsers, or a
  detached document edge case), the outer catch returned false but
  left the textarea in the DOM. On repeated Copy failures under that
  condition the leak accumulates. Finally-block guards the remove
  with `textarea.parentNode === document.body` so appendChild-itself
  failures don't double-throw on the remove.

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed
- Reversed-marker simulation: throws with clear offset-annotated message
- Normal-case re-run: byte-identical output (idempotent)

commits.subject: fix(pwa): always toast Check-for-Updates outcome + exhaustive switch guard
commits.body:
Two issues surfaced by an audit of the prior race-fix commit:

(1) UX gap when triggered from SettingsModal.
The original design had 'update-available' silent on the assumption
that the Update button in the home header would signal the user. But
the manual Check-for-Updates trigger lives INSIDE SettingsModal,
which covers the header — so a user running the check from Settings
got zero feedback on the one branch that actually mattered.

Fix: every CheckForUpdatesResult branch now surfaces a toast.
'update-available' shows "An update is available — tap Update in the
header" (4s duration, so the text has time to be read before
auto-dismissing). Toasts render at Z.toast (70) which is above
Z.modal (60), so the feedback is visible on top of SettingsModal.

(2) Non-exhaustive switch in handleCheckForUpdates.
Because the function returns Promise<void>, falling off the end of
the switch without handling a new discriminator value would silently
no-op at runtime — TypeScript wouldn't flag it. Added
`default: { const _exhaustive: never = result; return _exhaustive }`
so adding a new CheckForUpdatesResult value without updating this
switch becomes a compile error.

Docs:
- hooks/usePWAUpdate.ts: TSDoc updated to show the new toast text for
  every discriminator (was still showing the pre-refactor "silent")
- docs/TODO.md live-browser scenario #7: updated to match new toast
  behavior (6 toasts, one per discriminator)
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md: added a "Follow-up wrap pass on the undone
  commit" subsection documenting the race-fix commit AND this
  polish commit, so the session's final state is fully captured

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed

commits.subject: fix(pwa): dodge stale-closure race in Check-for-Updates toast feedback
commits.body:
The handleCheckForUpdates consumer I added in the prior commit had a
real bug: it read `hasUpdate` from React state AFTER awaiting
`checkForUpdates()`, but the closure captured the pre-check value.
During the await, a SW update event can fire → `_hasUpdate = true` →
listener calls `setHasUpdate(true)`, but React's re-render hasn't
flushed by the time the handler resumes. Result: user sees
"You're up to date" toast AT THE SAME TIME the Update button lights
up in the header. Confusing.

Fix: split the `'done'` discriminator into two values — `'up-to-date'`
and `'update-available'` — so the hook itself reports the outcome
instead of forcing the caller to infer from stale React state.
Read `_hasUpdate` (module-private var, always current) right before
the hook's return statement, not from the React view of it.

Changes:
- hooks/usePWAUpdate.ts:
  - CheckForUpdatesResult: `'done'` → `'up-to-date' | 'update-available'`
  - Updated TSDoc to explain the race-dodging rationale
  - checkForUpdates returns `_hasUpdate ? 'update-available' : 'up-to-date'`
- app/(app)/home.tsx handleCheckForUpdates:
  - Switch case renamed + `!hasUpdate` check removed (no longer needed)
  - Comment updated to reflect why 'update-available' is silent
- docs/TODO.md verification scenario: updated with new discriminator values

Verification:
- npx tsc --noEmit: 0 errors
- npm run verify:inline-scripts: 47 passed, 0 failed

commits.subject: refactor(pwa-icons): wrap-pass cleanups + docs + Metro quirk note
commits.body:
All 6 items from the icon cache-busting wrap pass. Nothing deferred.

## Fix 1 — Dead filter removed

`scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs` had
`.filter((name) => name !== 'icon.svg' || true)` — leftover from a
draft where I considered excluding SVGs from sw.js precaching. The
`|| true` made the predicate always true, so the filter was a no-op
that obscured the intent. Removed.

## Fix 2 — Preserve VERSIONED_ICONS declaration order

Script previously sorted PWA_ICON_PATHS alphabetically when
rewriting sw.js, producing spurious diff noise (favicon.ico kept
moving to the top of the list on every re-run even when contents
were unchanged). Now the rewrite follows the same order as
VERSIONED_ICONS in the script — human-readable, matches source
intent, byte-identical output on unchanged inputs.

Verified: re-running `node scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs` on
clean state produces zero git diff.

## Fix 3 — Legacy `assets/images/favicon.png` documented

Investigated whether `assets/images/favicon.png` (48×48 Expo web
legacy) is reachable. It's referenced by `app.json` as `web.favicon`
but that field is honored only by the legacy Expo Web toolchain;
synctone's Expo Router static-export pipeline emits icon `<link>`s
from `app/+html.tsx` pointing at `public/favicon.ico?v=<hash>`.
Left file alone (harmless, unused at runtime); existing CLAUDE.md
Icon Assets table row already labels it "Browser tab fallback
(Expo web legacy)".

## Fix 4 — docs/TODO.md live-browser entry expanded

7 new icon-cache-bust verification scenarios appended to the
existing High Priority live-browser entry:
 - Cold visit → every icon URL on the wire has `?v=<hash>`
 - Offline hard-reload → icons still display (precache hit)
 - Icon-change roundtrip: edit SVG → `gen:icons` → only the
   changed icon's hash diffs
 - Post-rebuild reload → changed icon fetched fresh, others
   cached
 - Android Chrome WebAPK regenerator picks up manifest URL
   change within ~24h
 - iOS Safari home-screen icon stays cached until
   Remove-App + re-Add-to-Home-Screen
 - `npm run build:web && npm run verify:inline-scripts`
   → 44/44 passing

## Fix 5+6+7 — CLAUDE.md docs

Added three notes to the Icon Cache Busting subsection:

- **Depends on Vercel Cache-Control headers.** The whole chain
  requires `manifest.json` being served with `max-age=0,
  must-revalidate`. Without that header the browser caches the
  manifest, so an updated `icons[].src` wouldn't reach the client.
  Same for `sw.js` and `version.json`.

- **Metro quirk: `process.cwd()`, NOT `import.meta.url`, for
  filesystem reads in `+html.tsx`.** Discovered the hard way —
  my initial implementation used `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)`
  which resolves to a Metro-internal bundled-chunk path and
  breaks the build with a very unhelpful "Failed to statically
  export route: _sitemap" error. `path.join(process.cwd(),
  'public', 'icon-hashes.json')` works because Expo's static
  export runs from the project root. Documented so the next
  person doesn't repeat the investigation.

- **Dev-mode caveat.** `npm run web` doesn't re-run
  inject-icon-hashes; hashes come from the committed
  `icon-hashes.json`. Mid-session icon edits without `gen:icons`
  leave dev serving old hashes. Non-issue for prod (Vercel's
  `buildCommand` always runs the full chain).

## Fix 8 — TESTING_GUIDE.md § PWA icon verification

New section matching the § Theme verification style, with five
step-by-step tables:
 - Build-time verification (automated via verify:inline-scripts)
 - Runtime cache behavior (Network/Application panels + offline)
 - Icon-change roundtrip (most important correctness test)
 - Install + OS-level cache (physical Android + iOS)
 - Manifest integrity (loud-failure contract)

## Verification

`tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors.
`npm run build:web` → succeeds.
`npm run verify:inline-scripts` → 44 passed, 0 failed.
Idempotency: re-running `inject-icon-hashes.mjs` on clean state
produces byte-identical sw.js / manifest.json / icon-hashes.json.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): icon cache busting — content-hashed URLs across HTML,
commits.body:
manifest, service worker

Adopts the glow-props PWA_ICON_CACHE_BUST pattern, adapted to
synctone's Expo/Metro build chain (vs the pattern's Vite plugin
reference). All 5 URL surfaces now carry synchronized `?v=<hash>`
query suffixes so a new icon deploy invalidates browser, CDN, and
service-worker caches on first page load.

## Part A — scripts/inject-icon-hashes.mjs (new)

SHA-256 8-char content hash per icon in public/. Writes three
artifacts:

- `public/icon-hashes.json`: source-of-truth map
  `{ filename: "8-char-sha256" }`.
- `public/manifest.json`: rewritten in place — every icons[].src
  becomes `/<filename>?v=<hash>`.
- `public/sw.js`: rewrites the PWA_ICON_PATHS array between the
  `@icon-cache-bust:begin` and `:end` marker comments with
  versioned URLs.

Idempotent: re-running produces a byte-identical output when the
icon bytes haven't changed. Fails loudly (throws) on:
 - any listed icon missing from public/
 - manifest.json containing an icon src not in VERSIONED_ICONS
 - sw.js missing the icon-cache-bust markers
 - manifest.json JSON parse failure

## Part B — app/+html.tsx reads hashes, emits versioned URLs

Reads `public/icon-hashes.json` at build time via Node `fs.readFileSync`
(Metro's static-render pass runs +html.tsx in Node with `process.cwd()`
at the project root — that's the reliable way to locate the file;
`import.meta.url` resolves to a Metro-bundled chunk path). New
`iconUrl(filename)` helper returns `/<filename>?v=<hash>`, throws
if the filename isn't in the hashes (prevents silent "leaked
unversioned URL" regressions).

Every `<link rel="icon">` / `<link rel="apple-touch-icon">` now uses
`iconUrl(...)` instead of a hardcoded path. The manifest `<link>`
still points at the stable `/manifest.json` — the manifest file
itself is rewritten with versioned internal URLs, and Vercel
serves it with Cache-Control: max-age=0, must-revalidate so the
updated contents reach the browser on every page load.

## Part C — sw.js precache + cache lookup

Three changes:
 1. CACHE_VERSION: v9 → v10. Old DYNAMIC_CACHE entries with stale
    versioned URLs get cleaned up on activate.
 2. PWA_ICON_PATHS array between markers is rewritten by the
    injection script with versioned URLs. Install precaches these
    versioned URLs directly (not the unversioned ones) so a
    request for `/icon-192.png?v=NEW` hits the precache on the
    new SW's first activation.
 3. cacheFirst regex allows optional `(?:\?.*)?$` tail — without
    this, `/icon-192.png?v=abc` wouldn't match the `.png$`
    anchor and would fall through to networkFirst.
 4. caches.match uses { ignoreSearch: true } as belt-and-braces
    so a slightly-off URL (e.g. during a partial rollout)
    still finds the precached entry.

## Part D — InstallInstructionsModal "icon looks outdated" disclosure

New `IconCacheNote` component in the modal — collapsible via
useState (React Native equivalent of HTML `<details>/<summary>`).
Closed by default to avoid cluttering the Install modal for the
95% case.

When expanded, explains that the OS-level installed-app icon
cache (iOS Springboard, Android launcher, Windows/macOS Dock)
keys off installed-app identity and isn't touched by the web
cache-busting layer. Platform-specific uninstall/reinstall steps
keyed off `navigator.userAgent` + `BrowserType`:

 - iOS (Safari, or Chrome-on-iOS): long-press → Remove App →
   re-add from Safari Share menu.
 - Android (Chrome/Edge/Brave): long-press → Uninstall → re-add
   via browser menu → Install app.
 - Chromium desktop: browser menu → Apps → Uninstall SyncTone →
   reload page → Install icon in address bar.
 - Safari macOS: Launchpad long-press → X → Safari File → Add
   to Dock.

## Build chain

`package.json` `build:web` + `vercel.json` `buildCommand` both
now chain:

  inject-icon-hashes → write-build-version → expo export

`gen:icons` additionally chains inject-icon-hashes so the
hashes stay in sync when icons regenerate.

## Verification

`scripts/verify-inline-scripts.mjs` extended by 15 assertions
covering the new icon-cache-bust surface (total 44 up from 23):

 ✓ every built-HTML icon <link> carries ?v=<8-hex>
 ✓ zero unversioned icon URLs leaked through
 ✓ every manifest.json icon.src has matching hash
 ✓ sw.js PWA_ICON_PATHS entries are versioned
 ✓ sw.js cacheFirst regex allows optional query string
 ✓ sw.js caches.match uses { ignoreSearch: true }
 ✓ all hashes match icon-hashes.json (no drift between surfaces)

Full build chain (`npm run build:web`) succeeds end-to-end.
`npm run verify:inline-scripts` (post-build): 44 passed, 0 failed.
`tsc --noEmit`: 0 errors.

commits.subject: chore(verify): JSDOM inline-script suite + DebugPill Rules-of-Hooks fix
commits.body:
Cold audit + genuine attempt at automating the live-browser gaps
I'd been flagging as unreachable from sandbox.

## DebugPill Rules-of-Hooks fix

Found during fresh-eyes read: the `if (typeof document ===
'undefined') return null` guard ran BEFORE any hook call. SSR and
CSR are different component instances so React doesn't actually
blow up on this, but it's a non-idiomatic pattern a linter will
flag. Moved the hooks above the early return; document-unavailable
branch now runs after all hook calls have occurred.

## `scripts/verify-inline-scripts.mjs` — automated verification

New JSDOM-based suite that runs the two critical inline scripts
(theme flash-prevention + pre-React debug pill) against a real DOM
with mocked localStorage + matchMedia, asserts their documented
side-effects on `<html>`, and validates the static DOM structure
of the built HTML.

Uses JSDOM's `runScripts: 'dangerously'` mode to execute inline
scripts naturally during DOM construction — same path a real
browser takes. 23 assertions across 8 scenarios:

1. No saved theme + prefers-dark → synthwave + `.dark` + data-theme
2. saved dark + combo=luxe → luxury + `.dark` + data-theme
3. saved light + combo=retro → cupcake + no `.dark`
4. invalid combo → falls back to retro/dark = synthwave
5. `__debugPreMountQueue` + `__debugPushError` wired up
6. `__debugPushError` queues + renders fallback DOM pill with
   HH:MM:SS.mmm-formatted timestamps
7. `<div id="debug-root">` present in built HTML
8. manifest + favicon.ico + apple-touch-icon + SVG icon links
   present in <head>

All 23 assertions pass. Run via `npm run verify:inline-scripts`.
Prerequisite: `npm run build:web` to produce the dist/ that the
suite reads from.

## Build verification

`npm run build:web` succeeds end-to-end against sandbox (required
seeding `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL` / `EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`
from the `UTIL_` env vars per CLAUDE.md). Emits:
 - 13 route HTMLs, each with the theme flash script (with the
   stringified COMBO_THEME_MAP intact), pre-React debug pill
   script, #debug-root div, and all required link tags.
 - 4-icon manifest.json with distinct purpose values (3 any + 1
   maskable at 1024×1024).
 - public/favicon.ico, icon-1024-maskable.png, icon-512.png all
   bundled.
 - @custom-variant dark + color-scheme CSS rules present in the
   compiled CSS.

## JSDOM, not Playwright

Tried Playwright first — Chromium binary download is blocked by
sandbox network restrictions. JSDOM gets us 23 of the 22 listed
live-browser scenarios that are actually automatable without a
real paint/layout engine. The remaining scenarios (no-flash on
hard reload, mobile-Safari clipboard fallback, iOS pagehide/
pageshow, HMR single-attach, prod visibility live-load) genuinely
need either a real browser or a physical iOS device — still
tracked in docs/TODO.md § High Priority.

## Deps

 +jsdom ^29.0.2 (devDependency; ~12 MB) — enables verify suite
 -playwright (removed; binary download blocked anyway; JSDOM
  sufficient for these assertions)
 (+@types/react-dom ^19.2.3 from the prior debug-system commit —
  was needed for createRoot typings)

## Unused-locals scope check

Ran `tsc --noEmit --noUnusedLocals --noUnusedParameters`: 16
remaining unused-locals repo-wide, ZERO in files I touched on
this branch. Pre-branch baseline was 31 — this branch's refactor
cleaned up 15 of them as a side-effect. Not adjusting synctone's
tsconfig to enable these strict flags (out of scope).

commits.subject: refactor(debug): wrap-pass hardening — prod visibility, URL redaction,
commits.body:
subscribe-during-replay, mount race, ARIA, docs

All 8 items from the DEBUG_SYSTEM wrap-pass landed. The biggest was
the prod false-positive: the React pill was gated `if (!isDev) return`,
which meant the pre-React inline pill's 20-second "React failed to
mount" escalation fired for every production user. Fixed by mounting
the React pill in prod too but rendering nothing when no errors or
warnings exist.

## Fix 1 — Prod mount-timeout false positive

`utils/debugRootMount.ts`: removed the `if (!isDev) return` guard.
The React pill now mounts in prod too, so `__debugMounted` flips
correctly, so the inline pill's 20s escalation doesn't misfire.

`components/debug/DebugPill.tsx`: added a visibility gate — in prod
the component returns null unless `entries` contains at least one
error or warning. Production users see nothing on a clean page;
when an error or warning is captured (via the inline pill's
`__debugPushError` bridge, `window.error`, `unhandledrejection`, or
intercepted `console.error`/`console.warn`), the pill fades in
with the error count + tap-to-expand.

`debugLog()` itself still no-ops in prod for ordinary `.debugLog()`
call-site tracing, so the prod log buffer only contains
error/warning captures — nothing gets logged speculatively.

Doc comment in debugRootMount.ts explains the tradeoff: ~10 KB of
inactive prod UI in exchange for actual error visibility to end
users hitting an issue.

## Fix 2 — URL fragment redaction

`redactUrl()` in `utils/debugLog.ts` now strips both `?query` AND
`#fragment`. OAuth implicit flow hashes, client-side session state,
and anything else after `#` is replaced with `#[redacted]` before
the URL lands in a `debugGenerateReport()` copy.

## Fix 3 — Subscribe-during-replay

`subscribeDebugLog()`:
 - Snapshots `entries` with `.slice()` before iterating so a
   concurrent `debugLog()` push doesn't inject entries into the
   "replay" phase of the contract (they'll arrive through the
   normal notify path instead).
 - Checks `listeners.has(listener)` each iteration so a listener
   that unsubscribes itself from inside a replayed call (by
   invoking its returned unsubscribe fn synchronously) stops
   receiving further replay deliveries.

## Fix 4 — Mount race guard

`utils/debugRootMount.ts`: new `window.__debugMountAttempted` flag
set synchronously BEFORE `createRoot()`. Covers the tiny race
window between `createRoot(...).render(...)` returning and
DebugPill's `useEffect` flipping `__debugMounted` — two concurrent
importers (HMR re-exec + transitive sibling import, etc.) would
previously race on `__debugMounted` and could attempt a second
`createRoot` on the same DOM element.

Declared on Window in `types/rn-web-style.d.ts` with doc
distinguishing it from `__debugMounted`.

## Fix 5 — Documentation

New `### Debug System` subsection in `CLAUDE.md` § Core Concepts
documenting:
 - The inline pre-React pill (what it covers, ~1.5 KB inline script,
   ships in both dev + prod)
 - The React DebugPill (separate root, pure DOM, prod visibility
   gate)
 - The debugLog store (API, capture surfaces, prod no-op, design
   choice to NOT filter React dev-mode warnings)
 - Why THREE cooperating surfaces: one per failure mode (bundle
   never loads / main-tree crashes / everything works).

`docs/TODO.md`: Live-browser verification entry expanded with 9
specific DEBUG_SYSTEM confirmations (throw-in-render test,
console-capture latency, `<div id="debug-root">` presence,
pre-React inline pill fallback, HMR single-attach, subscribe-
during-replay, mobile-Safari clipboard fallback, prod visibility
gate, URL redaction).

Removed one stale entry from the Low Priority section ("clean up
`as any` casts") — that was done in commit d314346.

## Fix 6 — React warning capture is intentional

Added a design-note comment in the `console.error` interception in
`utils/debugLog.ts` explaining that React dev-mode warnings are
deliberately captured unfiltered. Filtering would suppress
legitimate library errors formatted the same way; the right fix
for noisy warnings is to address the underlying issue.

## Fix 7 — Residual `PWADebugBanner` text sweep

Updated `hooks/usePWAInstall.ts` doc-comment and
`utils/debugLog.ts` doc-comment that referenced the old name by
file. `docs/SESSION_NOTES.md` retains a historical reference as
narrative (session snapshots describe past state; the end-of-
session rewrite will naturally replace it).

## Fix 8 — ARIA roles on DebugPill

- `role="tablist"` on tab container, `aria-label="Debug tabs"`
- `role="tab"` on each tab button with `aria-selected`,
  `aria-controls`, and stable `id`
- `role="tabpanel"` on panel body with `aria-labelledby` pointing
  at the active tab's id
- `role="log"` + `aria-live="polite"` + `aria-atomic={false}` on
  the Log tab body specifically, so new entries get polite-
  announced without interrupting other screen-reader flow
- `role="dialog"` + `aria-label="Debug pill"` on the expanded
  panel wrapper

## Verification

`tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors. All 8 fixes land without introducing
new casts or directives. Fix 7 grep confirms zero source-file
references to `PWADebugBanner` remain; the only hits are in
session-narrative history docs.

Live-browser verification of all items expanded in
`docs/TODO.md § High Priority` (9 concrete DevTools steps including
`throw new Error()` in render, console-capture latency check,
mobile-Safari clipboard fallback, production visibility gate,
URL redaction roundtrip).

commits.subject: refactor(pwa): harden visibility-based update checks + granular errors
commits.body:
Wrap-pass fixes to the PWA update-check work. All 7 items flagged
in the reflective pass addressed.

## Fix 1 — Initial-state gate at setup

`initUpdateDetection()` now checks `document.visibilityState` before
starting the hourly interval. If a user opens the app in a background
tab (middle-click, window-swap), we skip interval start entirely;
the first `visible` transition later fires an immediate check and
kicks off the cadence. Previous behavior ran the interval anyway
until the first `hidden` event — wasteful in the background-tab case.

## Fix 2 — iOS Safari PWA standalone (pagehide / pageshow)

`visibilitychange` covers desktop tab-switch and window-minimize but
iOS PWA standalone mode transitions through `pagehide` → `pageshow`
on app-switch (swipe-up). Previously we silently kept polling while
the installed iPhone PWA was in the background. Now:

- `pagehide` → stop interval (symmetric to visibilitychange→hidden)
- `pageshow` → immediate check (throttled) + restart interval
  (symmetric to visibilitychange→visible)

`pageshow.persisted` (bfcache restore) intentionally runs the same
path: the bundle may have been updated server-side since the page
was cached, so we re-check on every restore.

## Fix 3 — docs/TODO.md live-browser verification expanded

The existing "Live-browser theme verification" High Priority entry
absorbed the 7 PWA-visibility confirm bullets from this task. Now
one entry covers both theme + PWA visibility verification, with
full DevTools step-by-step instructions. Drops once verified
end-to-end on both desktop (Chrome) and mobile (Safari iOS
installed-PWA).

## Fix 4 — HMR flag ONE-WAY documentation

Added explicit doc-comment to `window.__synctonePwaVisAttached` in
types/rn-web-style.d.ts: once set to `true`, it stays set for the
session. Nothing clears it, which is correct today because the
listeners live at module scope and share the page lifetime. The
note spells out the cleanup expectation if a future feature flag
ever tears down PWA update detection.

## Fix 5 — CheckForUpdatesResult forward-compat doc

Expanded the `CheckForUpdatesResult` doc comment with a usage
example showing the full discriminated switch. Notes explicitly
that no synctone UI consumer currently uses the distinction, but
the typed return is ready when a future "Check for updates" button
needs it.

## Fix 6 — Granular typed errors (allSettled)

Split `'error'` into `'error-sw' | 'error-version' | 'error'`:
- `'error-sw'` — SW update threw, version.json succeeded. Likely
  offline or SW crashed.
- `'error-version'` — version.json fetch threw, SW update succeeded.
  Likely server-side deploy-in-progress or CDN hiccup.
- `'error'` — both failed. Generic catch-all for when there's no
  useful distinction.

Implementation switched from `Promise.all` (fails on first rejection,
loses the other outcome) to `Promise.allSettled`. Either side's
failure no longer masks the other side's outcome.

## Fix 7 — Rapid-cycle throttle

New `VISIBILITY_THROTTLE_MS = 30 * 1000` constant + module-level
`_lastPeriodicCheckAt` timestamp. New `runPeriodicUpdateCheckThrottled()`
helper: if the last check was less than 30s ago, log
`pwa:visibility-throttled` and skip. A user rapidly cycling tabs
(5× in 2s) now fires exactly ONE check, not five. The interval
restart is not throttled — only the immediate check is — so the
hourly cadence proceeds normally.

Chosen 30s specifically: well below the hourly interval (so a real
return-from-long-absence still fires the immediate check) but high
enough to suppress realistic tab-juggling.

## Verification

`tsc --noEmit` → 0 errors. All 7 items landed without widening
the hack surface (zero new `as any` / `@ts-expect-error` introduced).

Live-browser verification of the 7 scenarios (background tab, rapid
cycling, iOS pagehide/pageshow, HMR single-listener, native no-crash,
typed-error dispatch) requires `npm run web` locally — tracked in
the expanded docs/TODO.md entry.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): visibility-based update checks + typed checkForUpdates result
commits.body:
Completes the PWA_SYSTEM pattern's "Update Check Scheduling" surface
for synctone's custom-sw.js setup (synctone doesn't use
vite-plugin-pwa; hand-wired equivalents in hooks/usePWAUpdate.ts).

## Part A — Visibility-based update checks

Previously: `initUpdateDetection()` created an hourly `setInterval`
whose ID was discarded. A tab left open in a background window kept
firing `_registration.update()` + `checkVersionJson()` every hour —
wasteful and (for users with auto-sleeping laptops) led to phantom
errors in the debug log.

Now: interval handle stored in module-level `_updateCheckInterval`
so a `visibilitychange` listener can pause/resume it.
- Tab hidden → `stopUpdateCheckInterval()` clears the interval.
- Tab visible → immediate `runPeriodicUpdateCheck()` (tab may have
  been backgrounded through a deploy) then restart the interval at
  the same hourly cadence via `startUpdateCheckInterval()`.

HMR double-attach guarded behind `window.__synctonePwaVisAttached`
— Metro module re-exec on hot-reload would otherwise fire the
handler twice per visibility change. Flag declared on Window in
`types/rn-web-style.d.ts` (not `window as any` — that pattern was
already cleaned up in d314346).

Listener lives at module scope because the SW registration itself
lives at module scope; component mounts/unmounts don't control
this lifecycle. Native platforms skip the whole path via the
existing `Platform.OS === 'web'` guard at the top of
`initUpdateDetection()`.

## Part B — Typed `checkForUpdates()` return

Was: `Promise<void>` — callers could only call-and-ignore.

Now: `Promise<'no-sw' | 'done' | 'error'>` — exported as the
`CheckForUpdatesResult` type so UI callers can write a typed
switch-statement without re-deriving the string set.

- `'no-sw'` — SW API unavailable (non-web, browser without SW,
  or registration not yet initialized). Fast-path return.
- `'done'` — Check completed; `hasUpdate` will reflect any
  newly-detected update on the next listener tick.
- `'error'` — Check threw; logged via `logger.error` + `debugLog`.

Dual-mechanism check preserved (SW + version.json in parallel) —
the task spec's simpler `reg.update()`-only version would regress
bundle-only updates. Existing `_registration` null check gives the
typed `'no-sw'` signal early.

Existing callers (pull-to-refresh in `home.tsx` and
`DesktopLayout.tsx`) continue fire-and-forget — neither is an
explicit "Check for updates" button where toast surfacing would
help the user. Typed return is forward-compat for when that
button lands.

## Docs

CLAUDE.md § PWA System updated with two new subsections:
- "Update Check Scheduling" — visibility-based interval management.
- "Manual Update Check — Typed Result" — the three return values,
  what each means for UI feedback.

## Verification

`tsc --noEmit`: 0 errors (unchanged — synctone's been strict-clean
since d314346).

Behavioral verifications that need a real browser (tracked in
docs/TODO.md § High Priority for the next live-web smoke test):
1. Background tab for several minutes → DevTools Network tab shows
   no SW update requests firing at the hourly boundary.
2. Foreground tab → immediate check fires within a few ms.
3. HMR reload in dev → `__synctonePwaVisAttached` prevents a
   second listener attach (verify with `getEventListeners(document)`
   in DevTools console).
4. iOS/Android Expo build doesn't reach the visibilitychange code
   path (Platform.OS guard).
5. Manual check returns the correct enum value.

commits.subject: refactor(types): replace all hack-trigger patterns with proper augmentations
commits.body:
Honest self-review after the user asked "were these proper fixes?" of
the previous 14-TS-error cleanup. Four of those fixes were
cast-based (`as unknown as X`, `as any`) or minimal-directive-removal
rather than root-cause type fixes. This commit upgrades all of them
to proper module augmentations, and extends the same treatment to
every other `@ts-expect-error` / `as any` / `as unknown as` in the
runtime source tree.

## New ambient declarations — `types/rn-web-style.d.ts`

Module augmentations for:
- `ViewStyle` / `TextStyle` — `backdropFilter`, `WebkitBackdropFilter`,
  `WebkitOverflowScrolling`. react-native-web silently forwards these
  to CSS; RN's type declarations don't cover them. Native RN ignores
  them at runtime (no-op), so marking them on the shared type matches
  both-platform reality.
- `Navigator` — `brave`, `setAppBadge`, `clearAppBadge`, `standalone`.
  Browser-specific APIs not in standard lib.dom.d.ts.
- `Window` — `__pwaInstallPromptEvent`, `__pwaPromptReceived`,
  `__deferredInstallPrompt`, `__pwaPromptListenerAdded`. Globals set
  by the flash/prompt inline scripts in `app/+html.tsx` and
  `app/_layout.tsx`.
- `BeforeInstallPromptEvent` — Chrome-specific install event, moved
  from a local declaration in `usePWAInstall.ts` to global so every
  consumer agrees on the shape.

Scope discipline on the augmentation file: only properties that are
(a) genuinely used in synctone, (b) documented in MDN/caniuse, and
(c) silently no-op on native. No speculative API surface.

## Call-site cleanups (downstream of the augmentations)

- `components/ComposeBar.tsx`: `getGlassStyle` return type narrowed
  from the lazy `Record<string, unknown>` to proper `ViewStyle`. The
  stale `@ts-expect-error` on the <View style={...}> site was already
  removed in the previous commit.
- `app/(app)/chat/[id].tsx`, `components/ChatPanel.tsx`,
  `app/(app)/home.tsx`: three `@ts-expect-error` directives on
  backdropFilter / WebkitOverflowScrolling style props — all removed,
  styles now type-check natively against the augmented ViewStyle.
- `hooks/usePWAInstall.ts`: local `BeforeInstallPromptEvent`
  interface deleted (moved to the ambient file). Two
  `as unknown as Record<...>` / `as X | undefined` casts on window
  global access — gone; `window.__pwaInstallPromptEvent` is now
  typed directly.
- `app/_layout.tsx`: four `window as Window & { __x?: ... }` inline
  intersection types replaced with direct property access. Listener
  callback parameter narrowed via one targeted `as BeforeInstallPromptEvent`
  with a comment explaining why DOM's `Event`-only listener typing
  is too conservative.
- `components/PWADebugBanner.tsx`: five `(window as any)` /
  `(navigator as any)` casts on debug diagnostics — gone; every
  property now resolves against the augmented globals.
- `services/notifications.ts`: five `(navigator as any).setAppBadge` /
  `.clearAppBadge` calls replaced with optional-chained
  `navigator.setAppBadge?.(...)`. Runtime feature-detection
  (`'setAppBadge' in navigator`) retained as belt-and-braces.
- `components/MessageBubble.tsx`: three `@ts-expect-error` directives
  on View ref bindings (refs typed as `HTMLDivElement` because the
  file uses DOM APIs like `.style` mutation and `getBoundingClientRect`
  on the underlying element — this is correct for web, where
  react-native-web renders View as div and forwards the ref). Replaced
  with a documented `domRef<T>` helper that narrows an `HTMLElement`-
  typed ref to `React.Ref<View>`. One contained cross-type cast in a
  named helper with rationale beats three `@ts-expect-error`
  directives scattered at binding sites.

## Verification

`./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit` → **0 errors**.

`grep -rn "@ts-expect-error\|as any\b\|as unknown as"` across
`app/ components/ hooks/ lib/ services/ contexts/ stores/ utils/`:
- Zero `@ts-expect-error`
- Zero `as any`
- One `as unknown as` — inside the documented `domRef` helper
  body, which is the single narrow cast bridging
  react-native-web's View-as-div runtime reality to RN's type
  surface. Centralized, named, documented.

No behavior changes — all edits are type-annotation tightening
plus the mechanical call-site cleanup that follows once the
underlying types are correct.

commits.subject: fix(types): close all 14 pre-existing TS errors (strict-clean repo)
commits.body:
I'd been reporting "14 pre-existing errors, zero new" on every commit
this session and treating the baseline as someone else's problem.
That's the bit-rot pattern the "no skipping" directive rules out.
Closing all of them now so the repo is strict-TS-clean for the first
time.

## A. Supabase Edge Function (6 errors)

`supabase/functions/send-notification/index.ts` is Deno code, not
Node/Metro. Its `Deno` global and `https://deno.land/...` imports
can't resolve under the app's tsconfig. Fix: exclude
`supabase/functions/**/*` from `tsconfig.json`'s `include`/`exclude`
and add a minimal `supabase/functions/deno.json` so Deno-aware
editors still type-check it with the correct runtime. Edge
function behavior unchanged — Supabase CLI compiles it via Deno.

## B. `app/index.tsx` (1 error)

`JSX.Element` as a bare namespace no longer resolves under React 18+
strict types. Switched to `ReactElement` from 'react' (explicit import,
no global lookup).

## C. `app/(app)/chat/[id].tsx` (2 errors)

The `@ts-expect-error` on FlatList's `WebkitOverflowScrolling` style
was stale (RN types or React types now accept it implicitly via the
conditional-object pattern), so the directive itself errored (TS2578).
Replaced the directive + object literal with an explicit
`as unknown as ViewStyle` cast scoped to the web-only branch, with
a comment explaining why. `ViewStyle` imported as a type.

## D. `components/ComposeBar.tsx` (1 error)

Same stale-`@ts-expect-error` pattern. `getGlassStyle` returns
`Record<string, unknown>` which already accepts web-only CSS
properties without complaint. Removed the directive; added a
comment documenting why it isn't needed.

## E. Null-returning components (2 errors)

`MessageActionSheet` and `Toast` both declared `React.JSX.Element`
return types but had `if (!message) return null` / `if (!visible)
return null` paths. Widened both signatures to
`React.JSX.Element | null` — the actual contract.

## F. `hooks/usePWAInstall.ts` (1 error)

`navigator as Record<string, unknown>` — TS2352 because Navigator
and Record don't structurally overlap. Fixed with double-cast
`navigator as unknown as Record<string, unknown>` and a comment
explaining the explicit widening.

## G. `services/chat.ts` (1 error)

`sanitizeDisplayName(name?: string)` accepts `string | undefined`
but `updateChatDisplayName` passes `string | null`. Widened the
sanitizer to `name: string | null | undefined` — matches the caller
contract so TypeScript tracks the shape correctly. Body already
handled falsy inputs via `if (!name) return null`.

## Verification

`./node_modules/.bin/tsc --noEmit` → **0 errors** (was 14).
No behavior changes — only type annotations, config exclusions,
and a helper widening that affects zero runtime paths.

## docs/SESSION_NOTES.md

Known Issues section updated:
- Removed the "pre-existing TS errors untouched" caveat.
- New note stating repo is now strict-clean with a summary of
  what was addressed.

commits.subject: refactor(theme): single source of truth + prefers-color-scheme + tightening
commits.body:
Wrap-pass cleanup for the THEME_DARK_MODE work. Addresses every item
flagged in the reflective pass after the previous commit landed —
nothing deferred.

## Single source of truth (drift elimination)

Previously the combo → (light theme name, dark theme name) mapping
lived in three places — `constants/themeCombos.ts`, the inline
resolver in `lib/themeColors.ts#resolveThemeName`, and the
flash-prevention inline script in `app/+html.tsx` — each with its
own hardcoded literal. Adding a new combo would silently leave the
flash script returning the default, producing a flash for users of
the new combo.

Fix: new `COMBO_THEME_MAP` + `COMBO_IDS` exports in
`constants/themeCombos.ts`, derived from `themeCombos` via
`Object.fromEntries`. `resolveThemeName` now imports the same
constant (the old "circular dependency" comment was stale — neither
file actually imports the other, verified by grep). `+html.tsx`
imports `COMBO_THEME_MAP`, `COMBO_IDS`, `DEFAULT_COMBO` and
`JSON.stringify`s them into the inline script body. TypeScript
now enforces the three consumers agree.

## prefers-color-scheme first-visit detection

When no `synctone_dark_mode` is saved, both the inline script and
the `useTheme` load handler now fall back to
`window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches` on web
(native still defaults to dark because `Appearance.getColorScheme()`
would race against first paint). New `getSystemPrefersDark()`
helper in `useTheme.ts` centralizes the Platform guard.

The flash-prevention inline script mirrors this fallback with a
direct `matchMedia` check — same semantic, pre-mount.

## Verified sync assumption

Read Uniwind source (`node_modules/uniwind/dist/module/core/config/
config.common.js`) to confirm `setTheme()` is synchronous: mutates
internal state, synchronously calls `onThemeChange` + listener
notifications (including the rnw.js layer that adds the theme name
to `<html>.classList`), returns `void`. `applyWebAttributes()`
running on the next line therefore cannot race against Uniwind.
Documented in the `applyTheme` doc comment with a pointer to the
source location so future readers don't have to re-discover this.

## Honest docs

- CLAUDE.md § Web theme application — three layers: now marks
  `data-theme` and `.dark` as "currently unused / kept for
  forward-compat" instead of implying they have active consumers.
  ~30 bytes of HTML payload for each; the honest tradeoff is
  documented.
- CLAUDE.md new "Single source of truth" paragraph points at
  `COMBO_THEME_MAP` as the one place to edit.
- CLAUDE.md new "First-visit default" paragraph documents the
  `prefers-color-scheme` behavior per platform.
- docs/TODO.md High Priority: new "Live-browser theme verification"
  entry with 6 concrete DevTools checks the sandbox can't run.
- docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md new "Theme verification (web)" section with
  step-by-step tables for flash prevention, runtime toggle,
  color-scheme, `dark:` utility, and single-source-of-truth drift
  check. Added "Theme" row to Regression Checklist.

## Smoke tests run

- `tsc --noEmit`: 14 pre-existing errors (same count as before this
  branch's code changes); zero in touched files.
- `tsc --noEmit --noUnusedLocals --noUnusedParameters` on touched
  files: zero new unused locals/imports.
- Node `new Function(body)` parse check on the inline flash
  script with stubbed interpolations: syntactically valid.

Live-browser verification still requires `npm run web` locally —
tracked in docs/TODO.md High Priority as an explicit follow-up
with detailed DevTools steps.

commits.subject: feat(theme): complete THEME_DARK_MODE web integration
commits.body:
Closes the glow-props THEME_DARK_MODE pattern gaps found by the
pattern-audit trigger. synctone already had the Uniwind @variant
mechanism for theme-name-keyed CSS variables; this adds the three
web-specific layers that Uniwind doesn't touch: flash prevention,
`data-theme` metadata, `.dark` class, `color-scheme` root rule,
and Tailwind v4's `@custom-variant dark`.

## Parts A + E — Flash prevention inline script

`app/+html.tsx` gets a new synchronous `<script>` in `<head>` that
runs BEFORE the Expo bundle loads. It reads synctone's localStorage
keys (`synctone_dark_mode`, `synctone_theme_combo`) and applies the
theme class + `data-theme` + `.dark` class to `<html>` by first
paint. Resolution logic matches `lib/themeColors.ts#resolveThemeName`
verbatim and defaults to the same initial state as the Zustand store
(isDark=true, combo=retro → synthwave) when keys are absent.

Result: no light-flash when reloading with a dark theme saved, which
was previously visible because Uniwind's setTheme() only ran after
React mounted.

## Parts B + C — Web attribute + class toggling

`applyTheme()` in `hooks/useTheme.ts` extracted the web DOM side-
effects into a shared `applyWebAttributes()` helper that runs on
both the happy path (after Uniwind.setTheme succeeds) and the
fallback path (if Uniwind.setTheme throws). It:
- sets `data-theme` to the resolved theme name — keeps the runtime
  attribute in sync with whatever the flash script wrote, and
  future-proofs for any DaisyUI-native selectors that key off it
- adds/removes the `.dark` class based on isDark — enables
  Tailwind v4's `dark:` variant and drives the color-scheme rule

## Part D — `color-scheme` CSS rule

`global.css` now has `:root { color-scheme: light; }` + `html.dark
{ color-scheme: dark; }` at the top level. Makes native
`<select>` dropdowns, scrollbars, autofill highlights, and focus
rings match the active theme instead of defaulting to the browser's
system preference.

## Part F — Tailwind v4 `@custom-variant dark`

`@custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *));` added to
`global.css` right after `@import 'uniwind'`. Tells Tailwind v4
to resolve the `dark:` utility variant against the `.dark` class
on `<html>`. No current callers use `dark:*` utilities (the app
uses Uniwind's `@variant`-driven theme switching instead), but
having this registered means any future `dark:bg-base-300`-style
utility will work correctly without further config.

## Docs

§ Theme & Colors in CLAUDE.md updated:
- Architecture table now lists `@custom-variant dark`,
  `color-scheme` rules, `data-theme` / `.dark` attributes, and the
  flash-prevention inline script.
- New "Web theme application — three layers" subsection explains
  why all three (theme name class / data-theme / .dark class) are
  needed and which mechanism consumes each.

## Not verified

Live browser verification (no light-flash on hard reload, native
`<select>` dropdowns match theme, `dark:*` utilities resolve) can't
be done from this sandbox — should be confirmed with a local
`npm run web` + DevTools inspection. tsc --noEmit shows 14
pre-existing errors, zero new.

commits.subject: docs(claude): close glow-props fleet drift (align trigger fixes)
commits.body:
Five upstream rules that synctone's CLAUDE.md was missing after
glow-props updated:

1. § Communication — add "Work, not process." bullet. Keeps process
   opinions (branching, PR shape, commit granularity, etc.) out of
   work discussions since those are the user's domain.

2. § Prohibitions — add three new rules that extend the
   work-not-process principle into explicit "never" forms:
   - Never mention branches/PRs/rebasing etc. unless the user
     raises the topic first.
   - Never frame work as "out of scope" — work is either doable
     or blocked on missing input.
   - Never opine on git history editing / branch strategy / PR
     size / review flow / commit structure.

3. § AI Notes — add "All code is yours." as the first bullet.
   Every change in every tracked repo is the assistant's own work;
   don't hedge authorship, don't investigate prior changes as if
   written by a third party, read diffs when you need to understand
   a change.

4. § Code Standards → Timer and Subscription Cleanup:
   - Replace the local verbose version (prose + inline code block)
     with glow-props' canonical 5-bullet form referencing
     `docs/implementations/TIMER_LEAKS.md` for concrete patterns.
     The upstream pattern file now exists as a proper glow-props
     implementation doc; the code example lives there instead of
     inline in CLAUDE.md.
   - Reposition the subsection to sit directly after ### Cleanup
     and before ### Quality Checks, matching glow-props' ordering
     (Cleanup and Timer cleanup are adjacent topics).

5. § Documentation — promote from `### Documentation` nested under
   `## Instructions for Claude` to a top-level `## Documentation`
   section, matching glow-props. All doc subsections promoted from
   `####` to `###`. The previously-awkward `### REMINDER:` and its
   `####` children (Workflow for EVERY Session, External Reference)
   now sit at consistent `###` level under `## Documentation`.
   `## Instructions for Claude` remains as a separate section for
   synctone-specific User Preferences / Technical Guidelines /
   Important Implementation Details.

Re-ran the align trigger after each fix; all shared sections now
either MATCH verbatim (Process, Principles, Communication, Code
Standards Timer subsection, Triggers, Implementation Patterns) or
differ only by defensible downstream customization (the § Fetching
This File framing, which better describes synctone's role as a
downstream consumer of glow-props).

commits.subject: feat(ui): shared ModalBackdrop + haptic feedback across modal surfaces
commits.body:
Parts C (ModalBackdrop refactor) and D (haptic instrumentation)
of the APP_ICONS-maskable-fix task. Consolidates the 11 modals'
duplicated inline backdrop patterns into one shared component and
wires HIG-aligned haptic feedback onto every modal toggle, menu
item selection, and reaction pick.

## Part C — ModalBackdrop

Before: every modal in synctone wrapped its `<Modal>` content in
a `<Pressable className="flex-1 ..." onPress={onClose}>` with an
inner `<Pressable onPress={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}>` to keep
inner taps from bubbling. Each copy had slightly different
accessibility labels, no consistent z-index, and no single place
to instrument haptics.

- `components/common/ModalBackdrop.tsx`: new. `Pressable` at
  `StyleSheet.absoluteFill` with `Z.backdrop` and
  `accessibilityLabel="Close"`. Auto-fires `hapticLight()` on tap
  so every modal's dismiss-by-background is consistently tactile.
- All 11 modals refactored onto the sibling layout:
  `<Modal><View outer-layout><ModalBackdrop/><View zIndex:Z.modal>
  content</View></View></Modal>`. Content paints above the
  backdrop because `Z.modal` (60) > `Z.backdrop` (40). Taps on
  content hit content first (RN's gesture system gives later-
  rendered siblings priority for overlapping touches); taps
  outside reach the backdrop and close the modal.
- Affected: ChatHeaderMenu (dropdown + edit-name modal),
  SettingsModal, MessageActionSheet, ReactionPicker,
  MessageInfoModal, ScheduleComposerModal,
  ScheduledMessagesModal, ReportMessageModal,
  InstallInstructionsModal, ToneEditModal. TutorialModal left
  alone — it intentionally has no backdrop-tap-to-close (nav is
  via Back/Next/Skip).

Why the sibling pattern vs keeping backdrop-wraps-content: the
task spec's ModalBackdrop uses `StyleSheet.absoluteFill`, which
is a sibling idiom. Siblings also cleanly let `Z.modal` and
`Z.backdrop` participate in the z-index scale, which the previous
nested-stopPropagation pattern couldn't.

## Part D — Haptics

`utils/haptics.ts`: wrapper over `expo-haptics` with built-in
`Platform.OS !== 'web'` guard (expo-haptics throws on web; this
keeps every call site clean). Exposes `hapticLight` /
`hapticMedium` / `hapticWarning`. Errors are logged via
`logger.warn` and swallowed — UI flows never block on haptic
failures.

Instrumented, Apple-HIG-aligned:
- **Light**: reaction pick (ReactionPicker emoji grid,
  MessageActionSheet quick-reactions, ReactionBar toggle), menu
  item selection (every item in ChatHeaderMenu / SettingsModal /
  MessageActionSheet / ScheduledMessagesModal / ReportMessageModal
  / InstallInstructionsModal / TutorialModal), modal close /
  cancel buttons, modal trigger buttons (home gear icon,
  install/update buttons, compose-bar schedule clock,
  ChatHeaderMenu ellipsis, tap-to-add-tones hint), ModalBackdrop
  tap.
- **Medium**: long-press-fires-MessageActionSheet (both the web
  custom gesture path and native Gesture.LongPress). Confirms
  the 500ms threshold was crossed.
- **Warning** (`notificationAsync(Warning)`): destructive confirms
  — Clear All Data (Settings), End Chat (ChatHeaderMenu), Cancel
  scheduled send (ScheduledMessagesModal), Delete for Everyone
  (MessageActionSheet), Submit Report (ReportMessageModal).

`expo-haptics` pinned at `~14.0.1` (Expo SDK 54 bundled native
module version).

Verified: `tsc --noEmit` at HEAD shows 14 pre-existing errors,
same count as before this branch's code changes → zero new
errors introduced. Haptic testing requires physical device
(simulators don't reliably report haptics).

commits.subject: feat(pwa): dedicated maskable icon + multi-size favicon.ico
commits.body:
Part A (maskable fix) and Part B (optional favicon) of the
APP_ICONS-maskable-fix task. Closes the manifest-level `purpose`
reuse bug where /icon-512.png was listed twice (once for "any",
once for "maskable"); Android adaptive-icon was clipping the
tail glow on some mask shapes because the source wasn't designed
around the inner 80% safe-area circle.

- `assets/icon-source-maskable.svg`: new dedicated maskable source.
  Wraps the same chat-bubble + waveform content as the any-purpose
  source in a `scale(0.80)` transform about canvas center so the
  subject (including the 14-px-stdDev glow) sits comfortably inside
  the 409.6-px safe-area radius on every adaptive-icon mask shape
  (circle / squircle / teardrop / rounded-square). Same full-bleed
  `#09002f` background so each mask lands on the brand color.
- `scripts/generate-icons.mjs`: extended to render from both SVG
  sources. New outputs: `public/icon-1024-maskable.png` (from the
  maskable source) and `public/favicon.ico` (multi-size 16/32/48,
  packed via `png-to-ico` from the any source). Same 400-DPI Sharp
  render for crisp small-size output. Fails fast if either SVG
  source is missing.
- `public/manifest.json`: `purpose:"maskable"` entry now points to
  `/icon-1024-maskable.png` at 1024x1024 (was the reused
  `/icon-512.png` at 512x512).
- `app/+html.tsx`: adds a `<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico">`
  in the head for desktop browser tabs / bookmark bars / legacy
  browsers that don't read the PWA manifest.
- `package.json`: pin `png-to-ico` (devDep; build-time only — no
  runtime cost). Installed at ^3.0.1.

Why `png-to-ico` and not ImageMagick/manual ICO: pure-JS,
no system dependency, same Sharp-rendered PNG pipeline feeds into
it — keeps the generator deterministic and CI-reproducible.

Verified locally: `node scripts/generate-icons.mjs` produces the
full asset set; `file public/favicon.ico` confirms multi-size ICO;
`file public/icon-1024-maskable.png` confirms 1024x1024 PNG.

commits.subject: docs(todo): restore pattern triage decisions (task confirm item #9)
commits.body:
The fleet-alignment task spec (Part A) required:

commits.subject: fix(scheduling): service + hook correctness from review findings
commits.body:
- H6: cancelScheduledMessage now uses UPDATE (soft-cancel, status='cancelled')
  matched on id + sender_id + status='pending' with .maybeSingle(), verifying
  a row was affected. If the dispatcher beat us to it, surface "Could not
  cancel: this message is no longer pending" instead of a silent success.
  Mirror for dismissScheduledMessage on failed rows.

- M7: updateScheduledMessage drops the pre-fetch-then-update TOCTOU pattern.
  It relies on UPDATE + RLS + the BEFORE UPDATE lead-bounds trigger, mapping
  0-rows-affected to a friendly "no longer pending" message.

- M5/L4/L9: Added friendlyScheduleError() translating common Supabase error
  shapes (rate limit, RLS, constraint violations, PGRST116, realtime drop)
  to user copy. Used by the service on schedule/update/cancel throws and
  reused by both modals for their own catch blocks.

- L2: ScheduledMessageFailureReason narrowed to the canonical union
  ('chat_ended' | 'too_stale' | 'rate_limited'). The row's failure_reason
  is typed as `string | null` because unexpected errors write sqlerrm
  substrings; an isKnownFailureReason() type guard lets consumers
  switch-with-exhaustiveness on known reasons and fall back on strings.

- L3/M9: useScheduledMessages effects split so realtime subscribes on
  sender_id only (no churn on chatId change). Polling and fetch stay
  reactive to both. A new realtimeDegraded flag surfaces when the
  subscription channel drops/times out so the UI can show a reconnecting
  hint — polling still guarantees eventual consistency regardless.

- L4: snapshotReply propagates query errors instead of swallowing them
  into a silent null snippet. Also adds a defense-in-depth check that
  the reply target belongs to the same chat (RLS already enforces this
  via the SELECT policy; belt and braces).

- 'cancelled' added to ScheduledMessageStatus to match the DB enum.

commits.subject: fix(db): harden scheduled_messages migration from review findings
commits.body:
Addresses 12 migration-side review findings from the pre-merge sweep:

- H1/H2: dispatch_scheduled_messages() and cleanup_scheduled_messages()
  REVOKEd from authenticated — cron-only. Also re-checks the per-sender
  30/min message rate limit before INSERT, so SECURITY DEFINER can't be
  used to bypass the cap via same-minute dispatches.

- H3: INSERT RLS now requires chat.ended_by IS NULL on both sides so a
  user can't schedule into a chat the other party has ended.

- H4: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE wrapped in a
  duplicate-check so migration re-apply is truly idempotent.

- H5 + L7: Added scheduled_min_lead CHECK (1 minute DB floor) and a
  BEFORE UPDATE trigger that re-validates lead bounds against now()
  whenever scheduled_for changes. Client still enforces 2 min; DB's
  looser floor accommodates reasonable client clock skew.

- M1: Status enum extended with 'cancelled'; UPDATE RLS permits the
  pending -> cancelled transition; REVOKE of DELETE means cancellation
  flows exclusively through UPDATE. This closes the schedule-then-delete
  loop that would have bypassed the 10/hr schedule rate limit.

- M2: Dispatcher re-checks the reply target at dispatch time and blanks
  the snippet if the target was deleted between schedule and send. Up to
  30 days could otherwise pass between a user retracting a message and
  their content still being propagated via a scheduled reply.

- M4: Dispatcher JOINs chats into the cursor SELECT (no more per-row
  ended_by lookup).

- L1: Dropped redundant idx_scheduled_messages_chat (sender+status index
  covers the query with better selectivity under RLS).

- New cleanup_scheduled_messages() + daily 04:17 UTC cron prunes sent /
  failed / cancelled rows older than 7 days so the table stays bounded.

- Added explicit failure-mode comment documenting at-least-once
  semantics of the dispatcher's single-transaction batch (rollback on
  panic → rows stay pending → retried next tick).

commits.subject: feat(scheduling): add server-side message scheduling
commits.body:
Users can queue a message for future delivery via a clock-icon affordance
next to the send button. Survives app close / offline because dispatch runs
on the database via pg_cron.

Architecture:
  - New scheduled_messages table with RLS (sender-only), check constraints
    mirroring messages, partial index on (scheduled_for) for the hot path
  - dispatch_scheduled_messages() SECURITY DEFINER function + pg_cron
    '* * * * *' schedule moves due rows into messages; existing
    notify_new_message trigger handles pushes for free
  - Dispatcher handles: chat ended (failed/chat_ended), >1hr late
    (failed/too_stale), constraint violations (failed/sqlerrm)
  - check_rate_limit gains 'schedule' action type (10/hour)
  - services/scheduledMessages.ts: schedule/list/update/cancel,
    snapshots reply data at schedule time
  - useScheduledMessages hook with realtime + polling fallback
  - DateTimePicker wrapper: <input type="datetime-local"> on web,
    @react-native-community/datetimepicker on native
  - ScheduleComposerModal for picking send time, ScheduledMessagesModal
    for listing/rescheduling/cancelling
  - ChatHeaderMenu gets 'Scheduled (N)' entry on mobile; ChatPanel shows
    a compact pill on desktop
  - Tutorial gains a new 'Schedule Messages' step

Decisions locked in:
  - Cancel = hard delete (matches user mental model)
  - Modal-only, no ghost bubbles (preserves sender/receiver parity)
  - Native pickers per platform, wrapped in one component
  - 2min minimum / 30d maximum lead time, 5-min picker snap
  - 20 pending per user cap, 10 schedules/hour rate limit

commits.subject: fix(theme): use named import for Uniwind — fixes theme switching
commits.body:
The `import Uniwind from 'uniwind'` was a default import, but the
package only exports `Uniwind` as a named export. This made `Uniwind`
undefined at runtime, so every `Uniwind.setTheme()` call silently threw
a TypeError. The CSS class on <html> never changed, keeping className-
based styles (backgrounds, text — "pretty much everything") stuck on

re-renders in ALL components consuming useTheme().colors.

commits.body:
1. usePWAInstall: consumeEarlyCapturedEvent() race condition — was called
   inside useState initializer, meaning multiple component mounts would race
   to delete the same window property (second mount gets null). Fixed by
   moving consumption to module level (runs once at import time).

2. usePWAUpdate: offlineReady auto-dismiss missing notifyListeners() — when
   the 3-second timer cleared _offlineReady, sibling components subscribed
   to the module-level listener were never notified of the state change.

3. InstallInstructionsModal: 'unknown' browser type fell through to Chrome
   desktop instructions ("Click the install icon in the address bar") which
   is incorrect for unrecognized browsers. Added explicit generic fallback.

commits.subject: fix(chat): always auto-scroll to bottom when user sends a message
commits.body:
The auto-scroll logic in onContentSizeChange was gated by a scroll-position
threshold (100px from bottom). Layout changes from the compose bar resetting
or keyboard interactions could push the scroll offset beyond this threshold
after sending, causing the user's own message to not be visible without
manual scrolling.

Root cause: sent messages were treated identically to received messages.
When the user sends a message, they always expect to see it — the near-bottom
check should only apply to incoming messages from other users.

Fix: Track when the user initiates a send via a ref flag. The
onContentSizeChange handler bypasses the scroll-position threshold when
this flag is set, ensuring the user's sent message is always scrolled into
view. The flag is cleared after scrolling.

Applied to both ChatScreen (mobile) and ChatPanel (desktop).

Tags: bugfix, chat, scroll, ux

commits.subject: fix: merge OneSignal into sw.js to fix PWA install prompt on Chrome
commits.body:
Chrome showed "Add to Home screen" instead of "Install app" because
beforeinstallprompt never fired. Root cause: OneSignal SDK registered
OneSignalSDKWorker.js at scope /, replacing sw.js as the active service
worker. Since OneSignalSDKWorker.js has no fetch handler, Chrome didn't
consider the site an installable PWA.

Fix: import OneSignal SDK directly into sw.js via importScripts() and
configure OneSignal init() with serviceWorkerPath: '/sw.js' so it uses
our SW instead of registering its own. Removed duplicate push handlers
from sw.js (OneSignal SDK handles those). Bumped cache version to v7.

commits.title: merge OneSignal into sw.js to fix PWA install prompt on Chrome

commits.subject: fix: use network-first caching for JS/CSS so hard reload gets latest code
commits.body:
The service worker was using stale-while-revalidate for JS/CSS, which
returns cached content before checking the network. This meant hard
reload (Ctrl+Shift+R) still served stale app code from CacheStorage,
since CacheStorage is separate from the browser HTTP cache.

Changed to network-first: always fetch latest from network, only fall
back to cache when offline. Bumped cache version to v6 to clear stale
caches for existing users. Also fixed "banner" references to "button"
in docs and sw.js comments.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012nvsRGG6qvCXSbHiTPwmgX

commits.subject: fix: register service worker early in root layout for PWA installability
commits.body:
The service worker was only being registered when the home screen mounted
(via usePWAUpdate hook), which happens after auth initialization completes.
Chrome requires a controlling service worker before firing the
beforeinstallprompt event that enables PWA installation.

By registering at module load time in the root layout, the SW is available
before React even mounts, giving Chrome enough time to evaluate
installability criteria. The register() call is idempotent, so
usePWAUpdate in home.tsx still works correctly with the same registration.

commits.subject: fix: simplify PWA update logic to eliminate race conditions
commits.body:
The previous implementation had multiple layers of flags (sessionStorage,
statechange listeners, suppressUpdateBannerRef) trying to work around
race conditions that resulted from the fundamental approach.

The new approach is simpler and more reliable:
1. Register SW and detect waiting workers normally
2. On update click: send SKIP_WAITING, set flag, clear UI state
3. Reload ONLY on controllerchange AND only if user initiated

Key changes:
- Removed sessionStorage UPDATE_RELOAD_KEY hack
- Removed statechange listener waiting for 'activated'
- Use controllerchange as the sole reload trigger
- Single userClickedUpdateRef flag gates reload behavior
- Bumped SW cache version to v5 for clean slate

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YbKxdaTUQVeq8u4arrhstf
commits.title: simplify PWA update logic to eliminate race conditions

commits.subject: feat: migrate to three-color light theme from dark theme
commits.body:
Replace the dark SeaBlueCherry theme with a modern light theme featuring clean white backgrounds and three vibrant accent colors (Blue, Cherry, Teal).

**Theme Changes:**
- Background: Pure white (#FFFFFF) for main surfaces, light gray (#F8F9FA) for elevated elements
- Primary Blue (#055EBA): Main accent for buttons, links, primary actions
  - Added light tint (#E8F2FC) and border (#B3D9FF) variants
- Secondary Cherry (#BA055F): Magenta-pink for highlights and CTAs
  - Added light tint (#FCE8F2) and border (#FFB3D9) variants
- Accent Teal (#058B88): Tertiary accent color
  - Added light tint (#E8F7F7) and border (#99E6E5) variants
- Text: Dark gray (#1A1A1A) for primary, medium gray (#666666) for secondary, light gray (#999999) for tertiary/placeholders

**Component Updates:**
- Message bubbles: White text on blue backgrounds (own messages), dark text on light gray backgrounds (other messages)
- Chat background: Subtle light gray dot pattern (3% opacity) instead of blue
- Placeholder text: Updated from #A0B0D0 to #999999 (appropriate for light backgrounds)
- Read receipts: White checkmarks on blue message bubbles
- Activity indicators: Updated colors (#BA055F for chat, #058B88 for loading states)
- Deleted message tombstones: Light gray background
- Reply references: Light blue tinted background
- All borders: Light gray (#E0E0E0)

**Documentation:**
- CLAUDE.md: Completely rewrote Theme & Colors section with new palette details
- Tutorial: Updated read receipt description from "blue ✓✓" to "white ✓✓"
- Comments: Removed "SeaBlueCherry" references, simplified to "Blue/Cherry/Teal accent"

**Why This Change:**
- Modern light theme provides better readability and less eye strain
- Three vibrant colors create visual hierarchy and brand identity
- Clean white backgrounds make content stand out
- Better alignment with contemporary design trends (Material Design 3, iOS design)

commits.subject: fix: implement comprehensive notification state verification for mobile web
commits.body:
Problem: The 🔔 icon could show on mobile web when notifications wouldn't
actually work, creating false positives. Browser permission can be "granted"
even when service worker failed, push subscription expired, or system-level
notifications are disabled.

Solution: Added verifyNotificationState() that performs multi-layer checks
for mobile web:

2. Browser permission granted
3. Service worker registered and active
4. Push subscription exists (most reliable)

Impact: 🔔 icon now accurately reflects whether notifications will work,
dramatically reducing false positives on mobile web.

commits.subject: fix: prevent scroll offset race condition during pagination on mobile web
commits.body:
**Problem:**
Mobile web still experienced upward jumps when loading older messages, despite previous fixes.

**Root Cause:**
Race condition between scroll event handlers and pagination:
1. User scrolls to trigger pagination
2. onEndReached fires, loads older messages
3. During pagination, onScroll continues firing and updates scrollOffsetBeforePagination
4. onContentSizeChange fires multiple times with stale/updated offset values
5. Scroll restoration uses wrong offset → upward jump

**Solution:**
Freeze scroll offset values during pagination to prevent intermediate updates:
- Added isPaginatingRef flag to lock captured scroll offset
- onEndReached sets flag when pagination starts
- onScroll skips updates when flag is set (values frozen)
- onContentSizeChange resets flag after scroll restoration completes

**Result:**
Scroll offset reference point remains stable throughout pagination cycle, eliminating race condition and upward jumps.
commits.title: prevent scroll offset race condition during pagination on mobile web

commits.subject: fix: resolve auto-scroll race condition when loading older messages
commits.body:
Problem:
- When paginating older messages, FlatList's onContentSizeChange fired multiple times
- The isLoadingMore flag changed from true to false between callbacks
- This caused the second callback to incorrectly auto-scroll to bottom
- Users experienced unwanted scroll jumps when loading message history

Solution:
- Track newest message ID instead of relying on isLoadingMore flag
- Compare message IDs to distinguish new messages from pagination:
  * New message: newest message ID changed → auto-scroll to bottom
  * Pagination: newest message ID unchanged → maintain scroll position
- Replace setTimeout with requestAnimationFrame for more reliable web scroll restoration
- This eliminates the race condition since we're comparing actual data, not async state

Changes:
- Added newestMessageIdRef to track the ID of the newest message (index 0)
- Updated onContentSizeChange logic to use ID comparison for scroll decisions
- Improved web scroll position restoration timing with requestAnimationFrame

Fixes high priority bug affecting UX during message history browsing
commits.title: resolve auto-scroll race condition when loading older messages

commits.subject: fix: use native browser Notification API for reliable icon state
commits.body:
The previous approaches created a circular dependency between native permission
and OneSignal SDK, causing state flashing and unreliable icon display.

Root cause: Icon showed same emoji (🔔) for both 'granted' and 'undetermined'
states, and the code kept switching between native and OneSignal as source of
truth without fixing the underlying issue.

Solution:
- Use native window.Notification.permission ONLY for UI state (icon)
- Check synchronously (no await) for instant, accurate state on page load
- No more 'loading' state transitions or flashing between states
- OneSignal SDK still used for push token/subscription management
- Don't recheck permission when SDK loads (native is already correct)

This eliminates the back-and-forth between commits b03c99f and 84d2ce5.
Native browser permission IS the single source of truth and is always accurate
because OneSignal uses the same API underneath.
commits.title: use native browser Notification API for reliable icon state
